description: Version manifest for all governance templates and framework core files. Used by SETUP --upgrade to detect and apply template changes to materialized projects. v5.3.3 (fix/skillspector-hardening): post_review.py hardened after NVIDIA Skillspector scan triage (2026-06-11, 19 findings: 17 false positives documented in docs/project_log/validation/skillspector-triage-2026-06-11.md, 4 OH1 confirmed as hardening-worthy). Script contract change: dry-run is now the DEFAULT; publishing requires explicit --publish flag (--dry-run kept as deprecated no-op override) — post_review.py 1.1.1->2.0.0 MAJOR (breaking CLI default flip), README usage example synced (1.0.2). Review body (model output, untrusted) sanitised of control characters and handed to gh via --body-file temp file instead of inline argv (ARG_MAX + process-table exposure). parse_github_url tightened to [\w.-]+ owner/repo charset with anchored end. glab mr approve return code now checked (was fire-and-forget). SKILL.md Phase 6 documents the publish-opt-in contract. Also in v5.3.3: preflight.sh 1.3.3 — --json mode multiline-injection bug fixed (unquoted heredoc expanded $CHANGED_FILES inside a python literal; all dynamics now env-passed, heredoc quoted).
upgrade_strategy: Smart Additive Merge — ALL files use the same unified strategy: format-aware structural diff → add new content only → resolve placeholders in additions → preserve all existing content. No separate Cat A/B/C upgrade paths.

content_types.universal: No placeholders. New structural additions are merged additively.
content_types.stack_configured: Contains {{PLACEHOLDER}} tokens. New sections/fields are added, placeholders resolved via Smart Discovery + RDR.
content_types.project_data: Project-specific values. New fields/sections added with defaults, existing values never modified.

1.1.1: fix(IPP runtime binding) — adds Block 2 IPP Pillar 2 detector. PostToolUse Edit|Write to a governance artefact (docs/spec/*/design|test_plan|dev_plan|increment_plan|spec.feature|user_journey*|devops_plan|technical_due|qa_report*.md, docs/setup.md) is parsed; if filled H2 sections outpace `_progress.completed_sections` (>=3 filled with empty tracker, or filled exceeds tracker by 3+), a session-scoped `.claude/state/ipp-pillar2-${session_id}.marker` is dropped with `<path>\t<kind>` entries (kinds: `tracker-empty`, `tracker-lagging`). Consumed by governance-onprompt.sh on the next prompt as `<ipp-warning reason="pillar-2-violation">`. Block 1 (governance source watcher) preserved unchanged; detection conservative to avoid false positives. Lock-step with framework_core.scripts/governance-onedit.sh.

1.0.0: feat(EVOL-023): initial template — PostToolUse Edit|Write hook. Detects edits to docs/constitution.md or docs/setup.md and writes a session-scoped marker (.claude/state/governance-source-edited-${session_id}.marker) listing the changed paths. agent_templates._description: Per-role agent templates consumed by CODESIGN, BLUEPRINT (architect), DEVELOP, PO, QA, SECURITY, UX, and PEER REVIEW agents at feature time. Synced to materialised projects by factory-sync.sh under the same `.context/templates/{role}/` path. Tracked here so SETUP --upgrade can apply Smart Additive Merge with version awareness instead of factory-sync.sh's blind overwrite. Placeholders ({{FEATURE_ID}}, {{TIMESTAMP}}, etc.) are runtime-resolved by agents when generating per-feature artefacts — they are part of the template contract, not setup-time variables. Hence content_type: universal across the board.
agent_templates._architecture: Each entry's target is the same `.context/templates/{role}/{file}` path in the materialised project. SETUP --upgrade walks this section under Step 2 (Unified Smart Additive Merge) + Step 3 (New File Handling). EVOL-027 fase 1 — keystone for fase 5 (parser-canonical user_journey reaches MASS via --upgrade).

Reads docs/spec/{FEATURE_ID}/increment_plan.md § 1 increments, computes overlap of each increment's scenarios_covered/contract_surface with the change's affected_scenarios/affected_contract_ops, and transitions status per-increment according to immutability policy: MERGED never invalidates (surfaces a follow-up-increment recommendation instead), BUILDING receives pending_iteration + --pause requirement (preserves in-flight work), {DRAFT, READY, INVALIDATED} flip to INVALIDATED with invalidated_by_iteration + invalidated_reason stamps. Plan-level status flips to INVALIDATED only when every non-MERGED increment is invalidated. Mirrors invalidated_increments[] into dev_plan.md frontmatter. Wired into CASCADE_TRIGGERS for ON_CODESIGN_REFINE_ITERATION and ON_BLUEPRINT_SYNC_COMPLETE; ON_IMPLEMENT_SYNC_COMPLETE explicitly SKIPS (implementation-only changes do not invalidate increment scope). skills/factory-pr-review/SKILL.md.role: Factory PR Review — six-axis review (code, code↔docs, API contracts, ADR, traceability, complexity) wired as a LOCAL push gate (NOT a merge gate) plus an assistive PR reviewer for already-pushed branches. Phase 0 Coherence Audit (agentic, semantic) runs BEFORE preflight Phases 1-6 when the diff is governance-sensitive. Phase 0 reads `config/coherence-context.json` (single source of truth for `context: meta|downstream`), extracts changed symbols (renames, deletions, moved identifiers, public artefact names), builds a bounded collateral set via `git grep` against `audit.root_sets` minus `audit.exclusions`, applies six detectors (13 Reference coherence, 14 Placeholder leakage, 15 Manifest↔disk coherence, 16-meta meta↔template lock-step, 16-downstream code↔docs lock-step, 17 Commit↔diff coherence, 18 Bump severity↔change kind), and writes a session+sha-scoped marker `.claude/state/coherence-audit-${session_id}-${branch_sha}.marker`. Axis 6 (complexity) runs in Phase 3 when has_code AND config/quality.json present — invokes factory-complexity-check skill on changed source files, emits Block 19 (DC-28 Cyclomatic complexity exceeds project threshold). Hard blocks total: 19. Mode `--preflight` is the push-gate entry point invoked by the PreToolUse Bash hook; mode `--review {PR}` is manual and may publish to the platform with explicit user confirmation. Docs-only fast-lane allowlist matches CLAUDE.md Generation Standards §3 verbatim — every-path-in-allowlist exits 0 silently and skips Phase 0. Project-rule precedence documented in § Project rules and ADR precedence — the push gate consumes `branching.instructions.md`, `security_policy.md`, `testing.md`, `protected-code.md` + `protected-paths.json`, `codebase_inventory.json`, `contract-first-policy.md` + `api-standards.md`, `architecture.md`, `defect-prevention.md`, `coherence-context.json` (Phase 0), and `quality.json` (axis 6). Explicitly does NOT consume `review-policy.md` (BVL/IMPLEMENT 🔍 REVIEW hat scope, separate concern). ADR-driven exemptions via `pr_review_overrides:` frontmatter on ADRs under `docs/project_log/adr/` (project-wide) or `docs/spec/{ID}/adr/` (feature-scoped).

changelog: 1.3.3: fix — --json mode multiline injection closed (pre-existing, catalogued in 1.1.3 as out-of-scope). The output heredoc was unquoted so $CHANGED_FILES (multiline) expanded inside a python string literal -> SyntaxError on any diff with 2+ files; quotes/backslashes in branch names could also inject. Now ALL dynamic values (PRE_BASE, PRE_BRANCH, PRE_CHANGED) pass via env vars like PRE_FF already did, and the heredoc delimiter is quoted ('PYEOF') — zero bash expansion inside the python block. Human mode untouched.

1.3.0: feat — invokes check_dev_plan_task_format.py whenever any `docs/spec/*/dev_plan.md` is in the diff. The check enforces IMPLEMENT's task-format contract (`- [ ] [X.N]` checkbox vs orphan `### X.N` h3) — IMPLEMENT --build needs the checkbox as its single source of truth for completion tracking. Origin: MASS PR #331 retrospective (a real iter-7 plan shipped with 10 tasks as orphan h3 headers; all existing gates passed green because no script validated task format). Adapted from MASS capability 29 to the meta's `check_*.py` skill-bundled convention per ADR-0030 (capability-based architecture is MASS-specific, but the defect class is universal — every downstream project inherits the gate without further work). Inert in the meta repo itself (no docs/spec/{ID}/) but live in every materialised project.

1.1.3: fix(EVOL-025) defensive bash quoting around the JSON-output heredoc — `os.environ.get('PRE_FF', '$FINDINGS_FILE')` passed FINDINGS_FILE via bash expansion as a python string default. If the FINDINGS_FILE path contained spaces (rare with mktemp but possible), the literal would break. Now: export `PRE_FF="$FINDINGS_FILE"` as env var and read via `os.environ['PRE_FF']` only. Heredoc kept unquoted so other bash variables ($BASE_REF, $CURRENT, $CHANGED_FILES) continue to expand. Self-review mitigation during PR #16 audit. Out of scope (separate fix-PR): the `--json` mode `$CHANGED_FILES` multiline injection bug — pre-existing, unrelated to EVOL-025 path.

1.1.2: fix(EVOL-025) tally counter pre-existing bug — `BLOCKER_COUNT/IMPORTANT_COUNT=$(grep -c PATTERN FILE || echo 0)` produced multi-line value when grep found nothing, breaking the `[[ -gt 0 ]]` test below. Replaced with `grep PATTERN FILE | wc -l | tr -d ' '`. Surfaced now because v1.1.0 added the 0-findings code path (governance-sensitive diff + present marker, no other blocker).

1.1.1: fix(EVOL-025) marker key simplified to `${branch_sha}` only — drop the `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` lookup + the manual-invocation advisory branch (the empty-SID path was masking the gate during the first end-to-end push test, since PreToolUse Bash stdin does not reliably carry `.session_id`). Now: governance-sensitive diff + missing/stale marker → unconditional blocker. Marker keyed on commit sha alone — new commit invalidates; same tree across sessions reuses (identical bytes need no re-audit). Manual diagnostic invocation (`bash preflight.sh --json`) blocks the same way; users who need a non-blocking inspection can pre-write the marker. Lock-step with skills/Factory-pr-review/SKILL.md 1.1.1 + hooks/check-push-preflight.sh 1.1.1.

1.1.0: feat(EVOL-025) Step 0 — Coherence Audit marker check. After the docs-only fast-lane and before the existing Steps 1-6 (detect_change_type / docs_sync / OpenAPI / AsyncAPI / framework-aware), reads `config/coherence-context.json`, computes governance-sensitivity (any path in CHANGED_FILES under `audit.root_sets` minus `audit.exclusions`), and on sensitivity looks for the marker. Missing marker → blocker `coherence-audit-missing`. Marker present with `findings.blocker > 0` → blocker `coherence-audit-blockers`. Python detection block moved up to before Step 0 (was after fast-lane) since Step 0 needs `$PYTHON` for JSON parsing.

Two checks: (A) any `### X.N` task-shaped h3 must have a matching `- [ ] [X.N]` or `- [x] [X.N]` checkbox somewhere in the doc; (B) `status: READY` plans must have ≥1 unchecked `- [ ]` task. Accepts both list-form (`- [ ] [X.N]`) and table-form (`| [x] [FIX-7] |`) checkboxes. Output JSON shape matches check_docs_sync.py (severity blocker|important|nit, category, message, files_involved). 1.5.2: fix: refine greenfield detection. Old logic flagged any target with `CLAUDE.md` OR `docs/project_log/` and missing `docs/setup.md` as suspicious materialized-with-lost-setup, requiring the `--greenfield` flag to bypass. False-positive in the common case where a previous factory-sync seeded skills/hooks/CLAUDE.md on a target before /setup ran (Nexus-Tech-Link surfaced this). New logic: missing `docs/setup.md` checks whether `docs/spec/` holds feature directories — yes → genuinely suspicious (post-instantiation work in progress, setup.md lost mid-flight), error unless `--greenfield`; no → legitimate pre-instantiation state, auto-greenfield without flag. Header USAGE block updated to describe the new `--greenfield` semantics (override the spec-dirs guard, not the setup.md presence).

1.5.1: fix(EVOL-024): sync_skills() now strips the trailing slash from `Factory-*/` glob expansion before passing to sync_tree. Without the strip, `${src_file#$src_root/}` rel-path computation produced `Factory-pr-review//SKILL.md` mismatch and the strip yielded the original absolute path, leading to display garbage AND functional miscopy (every Factory-* skill showed as `+ added` and would have been written to nested wrong locations). Caught by the Nexus-Tech-Link dry-run before applying.

Multi-file skills (e.g. factory-pr-review) carry runtime artefacts beyond SKILL.md; SKILL-only sync left them inert in downstream projects (the push gate's preflight.sh, references/, scripts/check_*.sh would never propagate). Header doc updated: `.claude/skills/Factory-*/SKILL.md` → `.claude/skills/Factory-*/ (entire tree)`. Backward-compatible for single-file skills (no behavioural change for skills that only contain SKILL.md).

runtime_artefacts._description: Artefacts that materialise at SETUP --generate time WITHOUT a template source file. SETUP synthesises them from operational data (greenfield: empty seed; brownfield: scanned from existing codebase). Tracked here so SETUP --upgrade and CI can validate their PRESENCE in materialised projects without expecting a corresponding template source under `.context/templates/setup/`. Distinct from `templates` (which require source files) and `agent_templates` (which mirror agent-consumed scaffolds).
runtime_artefacts._architecture: Each entry's `target` is the synthesised location in the materialised project. `bootstrap_synthesised: true` flags that disk-coherence checks (factory-pr-review Block 15) skip the source-existence assertion for this entry. Versioning tracks the schema/format of the synthesised artefact, not source content.
